patch.value:
## Algorithm portfolio

### Execution graph
Topological layering; longest weighted path and slack; dominators; prerequisite frontier; transitive reduction; minimum cut candidates; resource-constrained scheduling inputs.

### Impact graph
Reverse reachability; bounded personalized PageRank or diffusion; articulation and bridge analysis; fan-in or fan-out; dependency cones; affected evidence and ownership.

### Knowledge graph
Weak and strong components by kind family; Leiden or Louvain-style communities with deterministic seeds; degree, eigenvector, betweenness approximations; structural similarity; redundant and contradictory paths.

### Temporal and multi-agent graph
Incremental maintenance; snapshot deltas; edge survival and decay; change-point detection; event-time windows; merge or federation boundaries; contention hot spots.

### Context selection
Graph-distance features; spreading activation; prerequisite and evidence boosts; diversity-aware MMR or DPP-style packing; novelty and redundancy penalties; bounded anytime assembly.

All outputs must identify edge families, snapshot, exact or approximate status, limits, freshness, and why each result matters.
Graph analytics for planning and context: critical paths, blast radius, bottlenecks, communities, redundancy, and explainable relevance signals
Implement deterministic, incremental, and explainable algorithms that turn typed relationship data into planning decisions and token-efficient context signals.

Order-bearing DAG analysis provides topological order, critical path, slack, prerequisite depth, frontier, dominators, and transitive reduction while reporting genuine cycles separately; Impact analysis provides reverse reachability, bounded blast radius, articulation points or bridges, dependency fan-in and fan-out, bottlenecks, and change-risk propagation; Knowledge-graph analysis provides connected components, strongly connected components by edge family, community detection, centrality, redundancy, structural holes, and orphan classification; Algorithms are exact where bounded and expose deterministic approximation method, seed, error or confidence bounds, freshness, and omitted counts otherwise; Incremental and temporal variants consume immutable history and support snapshot comparison, trend, change-point, and counterfactual what-if analysis; Each signal includes provenance and a concise explanation suitable for context packing; no opaque score is authoritative by itself; Evaluation compares quality, latency, memory, update cost, and token savings on scratch, medium, adversarial, multi-decade, and one-million-item fixtures
Derive actionable project and context intelligence from typed graphs without conflating associative and order-bearing relationships
Agents and maintainers can find critical work, impact, bottlenecks, communities, missing links, and the best bounded context automatically
Improves prioritization, change safety, duplicate avoidance, context relevance, and portfolio understanding from tiny to million-item trackers
A library of snapshot-aware graph analytics with explanations, incremental updates, approximation bounds, and evaluation evidence
The context engine already plans multi-signal scoring but needs trustworthy graph semantics and scalable derived features

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-13: context-engine items define relevance assembly and evaluation but no dedicated typed-graph analytics portfolio covering project planning,impact,community,and incremental temporal algorithms; this item supplies those graph primitives without duplicating ranking policy.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Start broad SDK relationship events, analytics, bounded graph-context, and conformance implementation bundle

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation start 2026-07-14: deliver the first exact deterministic analytics tranche over registered relationship semantics, including execution-order and impact/knowledge signals with provenance rather than opaque scores.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Execution analytics need an explicit precedence direction in each ordering-kind definition. An ordering boolean alone cannot distinguish source-blocks-target from source-blocked-by-target without guessing from labels.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation evidence: added exact deterministic ordering analytics with Kahn layers, frontier, depth, topological order, critical path, and SCC cycle reporting; bounded impact with shortest explanation paths; weak/strong knowledge components, orphans, hubs; and temporal snapshot comparison. Associative relationships are excluded from execution blockers through registry precedence rather than hard-coded kind names.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Remaining canonical scope after this PR: dominators, community algorithms beyond exact components, redundancy detection, incremental cached analytics, and CLI/MCP projections; keep feature open.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #556 review round 1 evidence: longest-path analysis now explicitly excludes cyclic targets reached from the acyclic prefix, and temporal comparison recursively stabilizes complete edge/payload key ordering. Cycle-entry and nested payload-order regressions pass; full repository coverage is exact 100/100/100/100.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #556 review round 2 evidence: stable temporal keys now honor runtime objects with custom toJSON semantics before recursive key sorting. Equivalent Date and ISO payload values compare unchanged without altering native undefined omission behavior.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Pause after PR #556 exact analytics tranche; remaining advanced/incremental/approximate algorithms and scale acceptance stay open.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Address PR #556 final review compatibility-documentation feedback

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #556 review round 3 evidence: documented the deliberate source-before-target fallback as compatibility behavior for legacy and JSON-parsed relationship definitions; focused SDK tests, lint, duplicate detection, CodeFactor rules, and typecheck pass.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Review fix complete; remaining canonical analytics scope stays open

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #556 review round 4 evidence: impact analysis now performs one BFS with parent-map path reconstruction instead of one shortest-path traversal per affected node. Regression coverage proves bounded and cyclic semantics and zero shortestPath calls; installed-package 10,000-node fan-out visited 10,001 nodes once in 27.28 ms. Full coverage is exact 100/100/100/100 across 5,681 tests.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Scalability review work complete; remaining canonical analytics scope stays open

author: maintainer-agent
message: Continue: pm graph analyze/impact subcommands surface execution, knowledge, and blast-radius analytics

patch.value: pm graph impact/analyze subcommands: CLI/MCP adapter over execution,knowledge,and blast-radius analytics

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation progress: execution/knowledge/impact analytics are now operable from the workspace surface via pm graph analyze and pm graph impact (CLI + pm_graph MCP + PmClient.graph). analyze returns node/edge counts plus execution (acyclic, topological layers, prerequisite frontier, critical path, genuine ordering cycles) and knowledge (components, orphans, degree hubs) summaries with a shared bounded sample limit; impact returns discovery-ordered affected rows with distance and an explaining path, honoring direction/kind/depth/limit bounds and truncation metadata. Regression spec exercises both envelopes, summary suppression, and bound enforcement on a live temp workspace.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation progress (graph-query-surface lane, 2026-07-17): three of this feature's recorded remaining-scope analytics shipped as public SDK primitives in new src/sdk/graph/analytics.ts — detectRelationshipCommunities (deterministic asynchronous label propagation, sorted sweeps, lexicographic tie-break, maxIterations/minSize bounds, convergence reporting), findRedundantRelationshipEdges (transitive-reduction scan joining each directed ordering/hierarchy kind with its inverse spelling in semantic orientation, per-edge bounded BFS witness paths), computeRelationshipDominators (Cooper-Harvey-Kennedy immediate dominators over the root's reachable subgraph with per-node gating weights). Exposed as pm graph communities/redundancy/dominators + pm_graph MCP + PmClient.graph with counts-first envelopes, sample bounds, and cost metadata. Remaining canonical scope: incremental cached analytics only (dominators/communities/redundancy now shipped).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Analytics tranche verification (commit a3b47c97): detectRelationshipCommunities, findRedundantRelationshipEdges, and computeRelationshipDominators shipped with exact 100/100/100/100 coverage inside the 5,842-test full gate. Live-workspace sanity: communities converged in 5 sweeps over ~3.1k edges (149 clusters, largest 134), redundancy surfaced real blocked_by/blocks shortcut triples with witnesses, dominators over pm-usfg ranked pm-8a2s as the 91-item gate. Remaining canonical scope on this feature: incremental cached analytics only.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR #580 MERGED (6b8ee9e4): communities/redundancy/dominators analytics are live on the public SDK and pm graph surface. Post-review upgrades: computeRelationshipDominators gained a maxDepth reachability bound with depth-truncation metadata, communities envelopes report iteration-bound non-convergence through truncated, and the redundancy/dominator index scans are Set-deduplicated. Feature stays open for incremental cached analytics only.
